children.heading: Operation and maintenance of navigation projects
children.label: a Operation and maintenance of navigation projects
children.text: Whenever necessary to meet the requirements of the Federal Water Pollution Control Act [ et seq.], the Secretary, in consultation with the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency, may remove and remediate, as part of operation and maintenance of a navigation project, contaminated sediments outside the boundaries of and adjacent to the navigation channel.33 U.S.C. 1251

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may remove and remediate contaminated sediments from the navigable waters of the United States for the purpose of environmental enhancement and water quality improvement if such removal and remediation is requested by a non-Federal sponsor and the sponsor agrees to pay 35 percent of the cost of such removal and remediation.

children.heading: Maximum amount
children.label: 2 Maximum amount
children.text: The Secretary may not expend more than $50,000,000 in a fiscal year to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Joint plan requirement
children.label: c Joint plan requirement
children.text: The Secretary may only remove and remediate contaminated sediments under subsection (b) in accordance with a joint plan developed by the Secretary and interested Federal, State, and local government officials. Such plan must include an opportunity for public comment, a description of the work to be undertaken, the method to be used for dredged material disposal, the roles and responsibilities of the Secretary and non-Federal sponsors, and identification of sources of funding.

children.heading: Disposal costs
children.label: d Disposal costs
children.text: Costs of disposal of contaminated sediments removed under this section shall be a shared as a cost of construction.1So in original. The word “a” probably should not appear.1

children.heading: Limitation on statutory construction
children.label: e Limitation on statutory construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to affect the rights and responsibilities of any person under the Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act of 1980 [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 9601
Buffalo Harbor and River, New York.
Passaic River and Newark Bay, New Jersey.

children.heading: Nonprofit entities
children.label: g Nonprofit entities
children.text: Notwithstanding , for any project carried out under this section, a non-Federal sponsor may include a nonprofit entity, with the consent of the affected local government.section 1962d–5b of title 42
